reforms.description: The Government has introduced a 150 per cent tax concession for R&D to stimulate research and development activities, alongside increased spending on public infrastructure projects and tax incentives to encourage investment, including accelerated depreciation allowances for plant and equipment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax concession and increased funding are designed to enhance R&D activities, which can lead to higher productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax concession and funding measures have been reaffirmed and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a significant tax concession and increased public funding represents a major reform in R&D policy.
reforms.source_quote: The budget included increased spending on public infrastructure projects and labour market programmes. Taxation measures included the introduction of new royalty withholding tax arrangements, the extension of the Fringe Benefits Tax to include employer provided parking and the maintenance of the 150 per cent write off for eligible research and development expenditure.

reforms.description: The Government announced the formation of up to 62 Co-operative Research Centres to promote joint research by universities, government, and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of Co-operative Research Centres is aimed at enhancing collaboration between sectors, which can lead to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The formation of Co-operative Research Centres has been officially announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance collaboration in research but is not as transformative as the tax concession reform.
reforms.source_quote: In 1991 the Government announced the formation of up to 62 Co-operative Research Centres designed to promote joint research by universities, government and industry.
